step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{A} We know from the evidence that ionizing radiation can cause DNA damage, specifically double-strand breaks, and this damage can lead to mutations if not properly repaired. While none of the articles explicitly mention "comet assay," the comet assay is widely known in scientific literature as a technique used to detect DNA strand breaks — precisely what ionizing radiation produces. Since radiation-induced DNA damage includes strand breaks, and the comet assay measures such breaks, it logically follows that it can be used to assess radiation-induced mutations.
raw_response: \boxed{A} We know from the evidence that ionizing radiation can cause DNA damage, specifically double-strand breaks, and this damage can lead to mutations if not properly repaired. While none of the articles explicitly mention "comet assay," the comet assay is widely known in scientific literature as a technique used to detect DNA strand breaks — precisely what ionizing radiation produces. Since radiation-induced DNA damage includes strand breaks, and the comet assay measures such breaks, it logically follows that it can be used to assess radiation-induced mutations.
